name: Casserole of Venison w/ Prunes & Pickled Walnuts
recipeIngredient: 8 prunes, soaked until soft and pitted
recipeInstructions:
1. The prunes and pickled walnuts provide just the sweet and sharp contrast that complements the flavor of venison so well.
2. Combine the flour, salt, and black pepper in a bowl.
3. Toss the venison pieces in the seasoned flour to coat them evenly.
4. Heat the oil in a heavy flameproof casserole over medium heat.
5. Brown the venison in batches, removing each batch as they turn brown and keeping them warm.
6. When all the meat is browned, add the onions to the casserole and cook until they are soft and translucent (about 5-10 minutes).
7. Stir in the red wine and water, scraping any brown bits from the bottom of the casserole with a wooden spoon.
8. Return the browned meat to the casserole, then stir in the pickled walnuts and prunes.
9. Cover the casserole and place it in a preheated moderate oven (350°F / 180°C / gas 4).
10. Cook for an hour, then let the casserole cool completely before refrigerating overnight.
11. On serving day, reheat the casserole again in the same moderate oven temperature for another hour.
